EnhancedContent:
*If you haven’t already, check out Arun Ulag’s hero blog “*[*FabCon and SQLCon 2026: Unifying databases and Fabric on a single, complete platform*](https://aka.ms/FabCon-SQLCon-2026-news)*” for a complete look at all of our FabCon and SQLCon announcements across both Fabric and our database offerings.*

The SharePoint Site Picker simplifies the process by replacing the manual URL entry with a browsable and searchable dropdown menu. By enabling site discovery directly within Fabric, it helps prevent URL format errors, reduces connection issues, and allows you to concentrate on building your data integration instead of searching through SharePoint.

![This is gif for showing all the SharePoint site picker functions](//dataplatformblogwebfd-d3h9cbawf0h8ecgf.b01.azurefd.net/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/this-is-gif-for-showing-all-the-sharepoint-site-pi.gif)

*Figure: SharePoint site picker functions overview.*

With SharePoint Site Picker you can use the same browsable dropdown across the following Fabric experiences and connectors:

- **Dataflow Gen2** — SharePoint Folder, SharePoint Online List, and SharePoint List sources, plus SharePoint destination.
- **Pipelines** — SharePoint Online List in source.
- **Copy job** — SharePoint Online List in source.
- **Lakehouse shortcut** — SharePoint Folder.

## Simplified SharePoint site selection

When you connect to a SharePoint source or destination, the Site URL field now includes a dropdown with two categories: **Recent sites** and **Favorite sites**. Select the dropdown, choose a site, and the connector populates the correct site URL automatically.

![This picture shows the SharePoint site picker dropdown experience](//dataplatformblogwebfd-d3h9cbawf0h8ecgf.b01.azurefd.net/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/this-picture-shows-the-sharepoint-site-picker-drop.png)

*Figure: SharePoint Site Picker drop down.*

## Quick search capability

When your target site isn’t in your Recent or Favorite list, type a keyword in the dropdown search box. The Site Picker searches across all SharePoint sites in your tenant and returns matching results in real time. Select a result, and the correct URL is entered for you.

![This picture shows SharePoint site picker searched results](//dataplatformblogwebfd-d3h9cbawf0h8ecgf.b01.azurefd.net/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/this-picture-shows-sharepoint-site-picker-searched.png)

*Figure: SharePoint Site Picker search results.*

## Next steps

You can start using the SharePoint Site Picker the next time you connect to SharePoint data in Microsoft Fabric.

- Explore the [SharePoint Folder connector](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-query/connectors/sharepoint-folder) documentation.
- Explore the [SharePoint Online List connector](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-query/connectors/sharepoint-online-list) documentation.
- Explore the [SharePoint List connector](https://learn.microsoft.com/power-query/connectors/sharepoint-list) documentation.

Have feedback on the SharePoint Site Picker? Share your thoughts in the [Fabric Data Factory IDEAS](https://community.fabric.microsoft.com/t5/Fabric-Ideas/idb-p/fbc_ideas/label-name/data%20factory) community.
Title: SharePoint Site Picker for easily connecting SharePoint data in Microsoft Fabric (Preview)
Author: Microsoft Fabric Blog
